bead.title: agent drain: idle watchdog counts tool-call/token activity as progress (D1b)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The service-drain watchdog cancels a healthy run when it sees no "meaningful" events for a window, but a long quiet tool call (build, test, or a slow/stalled model turn) emits no events it classifies as meaningful — fabricating a failure on work that is actually progressing. See cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go (the drainWatchdog idle path; re-pin line against HEAD — the meaningful-event classification + idle timer reset).

ROOT CAUSE
The idle timer resets only on events classified as "meaningful" (final, certain progress). Tool-call lifecycle events and token deltas during a long subprocess are not counted, so a genuinely-working attempt trips the idle cancel.

PROPOSED FIX
Count tool-call lifecycle events (tool_call start, tool_result) and token/progress deltas as meaningful activity that resets the idle timer. Bound the cancel only on PROVEN silence — no events of ANY kind for the window — not on "no events we classified as meaningful". Keep ToolCallTimeout as the per-tool hung-subprocess cap (separate concern).

NON-SCOPE: D1 route recording (merged), D1d tool-timeout config seam (separate bead).

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test in agent_runner_service_wedge_test.go: a stream that emits only tool_call/tool_result/progress events (no "final") for longer than the idle window does NOT cancel (idle timer keeps resetting on those events).
2. A stream that emits NOTHING for the window still cancels (true silence).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"root cause is factually incorrect in head: tool_call and tool_result already call resetidle() at agent_runner_service.go:364 and :383: The bead claims that tool-call lifecycle events (tool_call, tool_result) do NOT reset the idle timer and must be added as meaningful events. In HEAD (agent_runner_service.go lines 362-383), both tool_call and tool_result already call resetIdle(); they have been 'meaningful' since commit e0b7b9d53 (feat(agent): wedge prevention via loop-detection + meaningful-event idle reset). TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion (wedge_test.go:190) already covers AC-1's scenario. The bead is either obsolete or, if the intent is to additionally reset on progress/text_delta events, it conflicts with TestIdleTimer_ResetsOnlyOnMeaningfulEvents (wedge_test.go:79), which explicitly tests the opposite. An agent given this bead as written would have no coherent edit to make: the root cause is wrong, the proposed fix is already present, and the AC-1 test it asks to create already exists in substance.","fingerprint":"42b3853151a3452e4a2c84e5c14bc47179edb2acec92e0d54d31490243bbd63d","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"42b3853151a3452e4a2c84e5c14bc47179edb2acec92e0d54d31490243bbd63d"}
